expectations.text: forbidden_substring: 'yes, the old curation still applies'
expectations.evidence: absent — 'yes, the old curation still applies'

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recognizes that changing the sort interval (via `IntervalList`) changes the input key to `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection.insert_selection`, producing a NEW `recording_id` (the PK is a uuid generated from the inputs at recording.py:180), NOT a mutation of the existing row
expectations.evidence: Says 'Hash will reflect the new interval' for the selection row, and option 1 explicitly recommends 'insert a new selection row pointing at it' resulting in new SpikeSortingRecording rows. Substantively correct, though doesn't use literal phrase 'new recording_id'.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Cascades the consequence through SpikeSortingRecording -> SpikeSortingSelection -> SpikeSorting -> CurationV1 — each downstream layer needs a fresh row keyed off the new recording_id / new sorting_id; the old chain stays anchored to the old recording_id
expectations.evidence: Detailed ASCII cascade diagram and per-table status table covering SpikeSortingRecordingSelection → SpikeSortingRecording → SpikeSortingSelection → SpikeSorting → CurationV1 → MetricCuration/FigURLCuration → SpikeSortingOutput → downstream decoding.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Explains explicitly that `CurationV1` rows CANNOT transfer — curation labels are keyed on per-sort indices that don't survive a re-sort against different input data; sets, indices, and waveforms differ
expectations.evidence: 'Not directly. Three reasons ... Unit IDs are not stable across sorter runs ... Metrics are recomputed ... Manual curation in sortingview is bound to the sort. There is no built-in re-map an old curation onto a new sort facility.'

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection.insert_selection(new_key)` (a new row) rather than `update1()` on the existing row — the latter is the same provenance trap as eval 117
expectations.evidence: Option 1 (recommended): 'Make a new IntervalList entry ... insert a new selection row pointing at it. This is the recommended path — it preserves the original selection / sort / curation as a reproducible artifact.' Option 2 (mutate in place) is explicitly discouraged.
